strategy: Hit with Myth (+50%); Yoshinobu resists Storm 80%.

Never damage Yoshinobu with a spell costing 7 or more Pips. The Chain Reaction answers with a -60% Smoke Screen, a 650 Rebirth heal plus a 250 Absorb on the enemies, and a 500 Ice Frost Giant that stuns your whole team. Use cheap sub-7-pip hits.
Hisamoto Legion Shields the enemies on Round 1 and every 3rd Round after. Break or pierce those shields, or hit between them.
At only 1,505 health, chip Yoshinobu and the minions down with low-pip Myth spells rather than one big AoE.
If you must go through Hisamoto and Kensaburo, expect the follow-up Earthquake and Scarecrow chains on 7+ pip hits. Keep spells cheap throughout.
cheatNotes: On Round 1 the bosses take free scripted turns, spending no pips.
Round 1: Yoshinobu casts Mass Infection, cutting your healing.
Round 1: Hisamoto casts Legion Shield on the enemies.
Hisamoto re-casts Legion Shield every 3rd Round after. Pierce or break through it.
Hitting Yoshinobu with a 7+ pip spell triggers the Chain Reaction below. Keep hits cheap.
Chain: Yoshinobu casts a -60% Smoke Screen on you.
Chain: Hisamoto Rebirths the enemies for 650 and adds a 250 Absorb.
Chain: Kensaburo hits all enemies for 500 Ice and Stuns them.
After Hisamoto is dead, a 7+ pip hit on Yoshinobu triggers a new chain.
Chain (Kensaburo alive): Yoshinobu Earthquakes for 350 Myth.
Chain (Kensaburo alive): Kensaburo heals each Samoorai 150.
Chain (Kensaburo dead): Yoshinobu Earthquakes for 350 Myth and Scarecrows for 425 Death drain.
